questions.prompt: Why does iterating at the ad level (not ad-set level) preserve learning?

choices.text: Learning-phase reset triggers on ad-set-level changes — significant budget shifts (>20%), targeting changes, optimization-event swaps, bid-strategy changes. Adding or pausing individual ads inside a healthy ad set with multiple ads does NOT trigger reset. So all creative iteration can happen at the ad level while the ad set keeps its accumulated learning, avoiding the 4-7 day re-stabilization period.

questions.explanation: Ad-level changes don't reset learning; ad-set-level changes do.

questions.prompt: Account launches 1 new ad daily and ad performance feels random. Diagnosis?

choices.text: Single-ad daily launches never get fair exposure — Meta rotates impressions across the existing ad library, so a new ad arriving alone competes for the leftover share against incumbents that already accumulated signal. Switch to weekly batched launches: 3-5 new ads on the same day, 2-3 worst-performing paused on the same day. The algorithm now has a clean comparison set, new ads share fair exposure, and you get readable signal in 5-7 days.

questions.explanation: Batch weekly: 3-5 in, 2-3 out, same day. Daily-one launches starve new creatives.
